qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Consider the following program #include <stdio.h> int foo(int x, int y, int q) { if ((x<=0) && (y<=0)) return q; if (x<=0) return foo(x, y-q, q); if (y<=0) return foo(x-q, y, q); return foo(x, y-q, q) + foo(x-q, y, q);

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Consider the following program that attempts to locate an element in an array using binary search. Assume . The program is erroneous. Under what conditions does the program fail? var i,j,k: integer; x: integer; a: array; [1..N] of integer; begin i:= 1; j:= n; repeat k:(i+j) div 2; if a[k] < x then i

qids.prose: The average number of key comparisons required for a successful search for sequential search on items
